# Verifies the three comment scopes: line, file, and pr.
# Each scope corresponds to a different positional-arg form on
# 'gs branch comment add', and surfaces via the inline-list API
# the extension uses.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-04-05T16:40:32Z'

cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'

shamhub init
shamhub new origin alice/example.git
shamhub register alice
git push origin main

env SHAMHUB_USERNAME=alice
gs auth login

git add main.go
gs bc -m 'Add main' feature1
gs branch submit --fill
stderr 'Created #'

# Line scope: existing form.
gs branch comment add main.go:4 -m 'Line-scope comment.'
stderr 'Posted comment'

# File scope: path with no ':'.
gs branch comment add main.go -m 'File-scope comment.'
stderr 'Posted comment'

# PR scope: --pr flag, no positional argument.
gs branch comment add --pr -m 'PR-scope comment.'
stderr 'Posted comment'

# All three comments are listed; each carries the right anchor
# fields:
#   - line-scope: path + line
#   - file-scope: path, no line
#   - pr-scope:   no path, no line
#
# The text-mode output makes the missing-line / missing-path
# distinction easiest to assert: line entries print as
# "path:line", file entries print as "path:0", and PR entries
# print as ":0".
gs branch comment list
stderr 'main\.go:4'
stderr 'main\.go:0'
stderr 'Line-scope comment'
stderr 'File-scope comment'
stderr 'PR-scope comment'

# --pr with a positional argument is rejected.
! gs branch comment add --pr unwanted.go -m 'oops'
stderr '--pr takes no anchor argument'

# Missing anchor without --pr or --respond is rejected.
! gs branch comment add -m 'oops'
stderr 'anchor argument is required'

-- repo/main.go --
package main

func main() {
	println("hello")
	println("world")
}
